transcript.text: The note taker threw me off.
I'm like, there's multiple people here.
It's okay.
I just had so many windows open that I didn't see you and you surprised me like as if you had snuck up on me in our actual room.
It's just me so far, but Jacob, oh, there he is.
was about to say, Jacob's going to be joining us today, so you'll get a chance to meet him as well.
Nice.
Hey, Jacob.
Hi, Jason.
That is, I guess, my cue.
Sorry for being here.
I don't know.
I didn't mean to rush you.
No, no.
Is it just the three of us today or is Michael coming today?
I'm actually not sure.
I'm not in the office today.
I'm working from home, so.
You don't have your fish background.
I know.
Boring today.
I can do an intro.
Don't feel like I was on the spot just a minute late, so apologies for that last call running slightly over.
I'm Jacob Rudnik.
I'm a managing director here at GrowthX.
So I've been here since March.
foragelian.
I'm to be a age.
Thank
was kind of brought in to lead a lot of strategy and support technical SEO across a lot of clients.
So my expertise comes from blog content editorial, as well as technical SEO, little bit of link building as well.
I'm one of, I don't know, half a dozen or so managing directors across our different accounts.
And we, well, different skill sets.
So you'll see other folks from time to time as like depending on needs and where we connect in.
So, yeah, I can go with my, a little bit of my background.
so started at my content SaaS journey at G2, which was G2 Crowded at the time and just like raised the Series A.
So I got really lucky.
went to a company that worked really well and like learned content SEO, kind of very hands-on.
And since then I've led content and SEO strategies like ActiveCampaign, Scribe, Software.
So all B2B SaaS primarily.
And then here, B2B runs a lot of like the day-to-day and week-to-week operations, really great on the.

So quality standpoint, like I have experience there, but spend less of that time, I'd end up working, like we connect a ton on the longer vision for your content strategy, the different, like clusters with also different content types, a lot of work on like reporting and other things.

And then I think where we get pulled in, so this is like an open-ended invitation to some extent, like we obviously have limitations and things, but we end up supporting like, again, mentioned technical SEO, things on the site, like how do we set up content and information architecture, you know, both with what we're doing and that we want our content to be connected to a website that's working really well.
So we're often supporting their strategy on like, you as we move further into our journey together, conversion rate optimization, right?
Like right now we're just trying to get basic clicks, but ultimately the goal is going to be to drive revenue, leads, et cetera, for you.
So we want to make the content work with traffic and then turn it into business results for you.
So we spend a lot of time there, you know, two, three months down the road.
That starts to pick up.
Probably some other things as well.
It's really ad hoc.
I know some clients are just like, I'm doing influencer campaigns.
Who knows about this and can support?
We fly in and give what experience we have.
We try to create documentation that we can share when it's relevant.
So we're always building as we go.
Whatever your random marketing SEO website needs are, we support it as best we can, bring in experts across the company.
So I do a lot of that.
But also, again, there are other faces that I'm sure you'll meet as things go.
But I wanted to join today.
Definitely see how things are going, chime in where necessary, and we'll continue to do that, obviously.
But also call some of that.
We'll have a lot of work together as we go and grow together.
Awesome.
That's super helpful.
Thanks for all that background.
Yeah, for sure.
Maybe I can just spend a moment.
I'll just kind of give you my background, too.
So my background's in design, mostly with startups.
I've spent most of my career working with really early stage startups.
I've
And so I've been able to kind of design and build a bit on the Webflow front.
I'm not highly technical, but I've owned a bunch of different websites and I'm usually just kind of scrappy, throwing it together, done some editorial stuff on these sites.
But my expertise is not deep in SEO or marketing or optimization, any of these things.
So I'm super glad to have you guys, you know, helping anything you see that's like, Jason, like this would be better.
I'm all ears that that's I appreciate your help with all that stuff.
Yeah, for sure.
I think that's where we can be a really great arm for like a company like yours.
And I mean, Series A and sort of like, I don't know, Series A, but like all like service, that's my favorite place to be.
Like when I went to ActiveCampaign and we were Series D and 800 people, like I hated that, you know, rigidity.
Like Scribe 30 people, Softer 40, G2 when it was 30, like those are where I love to just like go roll up the sleeves.
So we'll definitely share those.
I'm definitely just getting myself acquainted to your site, business, et cetera.
But there will be tons that we can apply that we've seen and I've seen.
So happy to do those and glad we can just move fast together.
Yeah, correct.
I'll just share the agenda.
We don't have too much that we have to cover today.
So it's great to have Jakub here.

And it's a great opportunity, too, now that we've published some content and have some stuff live, if there are things you're seeing in terms of editorial quality or just kind of any high-level feedback you have to make sure that's captured as well.
The one ops thing I wanted to mention is, so we have been looking into this table issue.
So I know tables, I talked to a bunch of the other managing editors, and tables are tricky on Webflow for some reason.
But Suleiman, our publishing ops specialist, has said that he's always been able to get it to work with the other Webflow CMS.
And it's gases that it has something to do.
With the CSS on your site specifically.
So I don't know if that's something you kind of want to look into.
It sounds like that's his best guess based on what's going on because it's okay on mobile.
It's okay on desktop.
It looks like it's breaking when you sort of adjust the size of the desktop window to a certain size.
So his best guess is it's in your CSS.
So I think it's worth looking into.

I also think that if, you know, tables are finicky on Webflow, I know that other managing editors have just said that with Webflow clients, they just kind of try to avoid tables unless it's kind of strictly necessary for the content, which we can do as well.
Yeah, I lean towards that.
I'm happy to keep digging on this too.
But yeah, I think just any table that is, you know, five columns wide is going to not look great on a phone.
Like you need to do something responsive.
And I think with the, you know, typically in Webflow, if I'm building a table, I can completely, you know, change it and make it look anyway.
I want, but with the CMS, it's a little trickier.
And we, you know, I noticed, like, I looked at the embedded code that got dropped into CMS, and it's literally just a coded up table with, you know, it's got minimum width, 800.
Like, it's not going to work.
We need to make some big changes to it.
So anyway, we can keep looking at it.
But yeah, my gut says let's not have huge tables in there.
And if we can just use bulleted lists or whatever, kind of keep it simple.
So we can definitely do that.

And we can also go, if we can't get it to look better, we can go back and that piece of content, like, rerun it in a different format and just kind of avoid that kind of specific template type of content going forward as well.
Yeah.
Like a table that's maybe two or three columns I can see working, but it's one that's five.
It's just, it's big.
Another thing, too, I'd mention that, but I'm actually okay with a little side scrolling.
If we could keep the.
Age from breaking, but like, yeah, the whole layout's like breaking, so that's not going to work.
Yeah, yeah, I think that's definitely the issue is the table is actually okay, but yeah, the way that it's breaking the format of the blog post, so.
Yeah, yeah, it's got a minimum width, like I said, it's like, we could try removing that and just see if it flexes, but yeah.

Yeah, okay, so maybe we'll go back and see if we could adjust that table, even if to make it something that's not quite so wide on our end as well, and just replace it with something different and see if that helps with the page breaking issue.
Yeah, yeah, even just like, like typically on mobile for a table, I would just do like stacked cards.
Yeah.
So maybe we just format it that way for all screens.
Yeah, okay, well, we'll figure that out.
We'll see if we can, yeah, just change that and drop in something different on our end to keep it from doing that.
Cool, okay.
And then in terms of other content updates, we have a good runway right now.
We articles.
think we have a couple weeks set up in there.
Two that are going to stage today.
There's four more in production coming your way today and tomorrow.
So if you have any kind of high-level feedback that you're noticing, like now's a great time to let us know.
Yeah, no, high-level, I think it's really good.
I've been happy with the quality of the article.
You know, find myself like, I've been reading the whole thing word for word, and I'm like, gosh, like I often don't have anything to add.
The only thing that I noticed in particular yesterday, some of the capabilities I think are a little oversold in terms of expense reporting and management.
Like we just don't have some of those features.
So I just called those out.
So, and then, you know, maybe just moving forward, we can, you know, dial that back a little bit and what we're promising in terms of the feature set.
So, yeah, we can, when you add those comments, we'll like have.
And we can use that to update our artifacts that go more specifically into the features.
So hopefully, once you comment it once, you hopefully shouldn't have to keep commenting on the same things.
Cool.
Okay.
But yeah, it's looking good.
Great.
Well, I added the link to the Looker dashboard just so you can take a look.
And I know Scratch you don't have access to, so I tried to just start including just a screenshot of the dashboard so you can see a little bit more.
Obviously, it's very early days.
But my goal is to sort of, you know, in these weekly syncs, just give you like a peek at anything that's going on now.
And I think it'll be more useful going forward when we have, you know, more data to collect.
Awesome.
Yeah.
And I know I had messed up on the setting for some of those.
I think three of the original posts weren't being indexed.
So I know we just are now getting those rolling soon.
Well, now we know.
Now it's good.
Yeah, it should be good.
Should be going towards the future.
Those were my test pages that I just forgot to change the settings on when I published.
Yes, I think it's all good.
Once that got removed, we weren't getting that error message for indexing anymore.
So we should be good to go now.
Perfect.
Awesome.
Well, I think that's all from my end on content updates.
I don't know if either of you guys had anything else you wanted to talk through.
I don't think so.
Are we still, I know we just rescheduled this meeting for a different time.
Are we still meeting every week or are we dialing that back now that we're in phase two?
What's the right frequency to meet?
Yeah, that's a great question.
I mean, we are happy to keep meeting with you weekly and giving you updates.

If things are sort of humming along and you don't have any big feedback and we don't have anything specific to discuss and you want the time back, I think we can also change the cadence to sometimes doing async or just put all of this in.
notion as well.
I think it's kind of up to you.
Okay.
I do like checking in.
I think it's nice to have a quick conversation, even if, you know, like today, it only goes 10 or 15 minutes.
That's totally fine.
And in the future, if, you know, if we do need to cancel or something, I'll let you guys know.
But okay.
Yeah, let's keep just checking in.
Okay.
That sounds great.
All right.
Awesome.
Good to meet you, Jakub.
Hey, great to meet you as well.
And I, you know, we were just wrapping really quickly.
So I'm sorry to step us back, but just look at the GSC and the, you can look at it in Looker as well.
But I mean, it's the earliest of signals, but this is what we want to see.
We want to just see impressions up into the right, like obviously start to see these clicks, which we've seen, you know, our first handful.
But that's the impressions, especially over this first like month, six weeks, that's where I want to see.
There will be a week where it, you know, dips slightly or something, but as long as this continues up into the right, that's
I wanted to be watching over month one, month two, and then see those move from impressions to clicks.
And so the other thing I'm looking at is kind of a coverage across all of our articles.
We have one article that's getting all the impressions and clicks.
It's great to have one successful one, but we do want to see some breadth there and make sure we're getting a lot of shots in goal that are having somewhat success.
So watching these, things look really normal right now, especially, like, jump-starting content from zero to one, you know, that, like, this looks good.
But we'll be watching it more and have more insights over follow-in-x.
Just want to, like, that's what I'm looking for at this stage, and things look healthy, especially since we just turned on, like, the indexing for a few of those.
But all minor considerations.
Sweet.
Yeah, that's exciting.
That's it for my end.
Sorry to jump us back in a second.
Yeah, appreciate that.
All right.
All right.
Well, thanks, everyone, and we'll see you next week.
All right.
Thanks, man.

default_summary.markdown_formatted:
## Meeting Purpose

[Introduce Jakub, review content, and discuss operational issues.](https://fathom.video/share/hnjbqbx-s2xK3UzTADLPqkTzoZ6sf6cB?tab=summary&timestamp=315.0)

## Key Takeaways

  - [Jakub Rudnik (GrowthX Managing Director) joined to lead strategy, technical SEO, and long-term content vision.](https://fathom.video/share/hnjbqbx-s2xK3UzTADLPqkTzoZ6sf6cB?tab=summary&timestamp=43.0)
  - [A 5-column table is breaking the blog layout; the fix is to replace it with a simpler format (e.g., stacked cards) and avoid complex tables going forward.](https://fathom.video/share/hnjbqbx-s2xK3UzTADLPqkTzoZ6sf6cB?tab=summary&timestamp=337.0)
  - [Content quality is high, but future articles must avoid "overselling" features (e.g., expense reporting) to align with current product capabilities.](https://fathom.video/share/hnjbqbx-s2xK3UzTADLPqkTzoZ6sf6cB?tab=summary&timestamp=552.0)
  - [Early SEO data is healthy (impressions are growing), but the focus is on distributing traffic across all articles, not just one.](https://fathom.video/share/hnjbqbx-s2xK3UzTADLPqkTzoZ6sf6cB?tab=summary&timestamp=748.0)

## Topics

### Team & Roles

  - [**Jakub Rudnik (GrowthX):** Managing Director now leading strategy, technical SEO, and long-term content vision.](https://fathom.video/share/hnjbqbx-s2xK3UzTADLPqkTzoZ6sf6cB?tab=summary&timestamp=43.0)
      - [**Background:** B2B SaaS content/SEO at G2, ActiveCampaign, Scribe, and Softer.](https://fathom.video/share/hnjbqbx-s2xK3UzTADLPqkTzoZ6sf6cB?tab=summary&timestamp=88.0)
      - [**Focus:** Content clusters, reporting, and future CRO.](https://fathom.video/share/hnjbqbx-s2xK3UzTADLPqkTzoZ6sf6cB?tab=summary&timestamp=113.0)
  - [**Jason Flateboe (Otto):** Founder with a design/Webflow background; seeks GrowthX's expertise for marketing/SEO.](https://fathom.video/share/hnjbqbx-s2xK3UzTADLPqkTzoZ6sf6cB?tab=summary&timestamp=228.0)

### Content Operations

  - [**Webflow Table Issue:** A 5-column table breaks the blog layout on desktop, likely due to a `min-width: 800px` style.](https://fathom.video/share/hnjbqbx-s2xK3UzTADLPqkTzoZ6sf6cB?tab=summary&timestamp=337.0)
      - [**Solution:** Replace the table with a simpler format (e.g., stacked cards) and avoid complex tables in future content.](https://fathom.video/share/hnjbqbx-s2xK3UzTADLPqkTzoZ6sf6cB?tab=summary&timestamp=337.0)
  - [**Content Pipeline:**](https://fathom.video/share/hnjbqbx-s2xK3UzTADLPqkTzoZ6sf6cB?tab=summary&timestamp=536.0)
      - [**Staging:** 2 articles today.](https://fathom.video/share/hnjbqbx-s2xK3UzTADLPqkTzoZ6sf6cB?tab=summary&timestamp=536.0)
      - [**Production:** 4 articles by tomorrow.](https://fathom.video/share/hnjbqbx-s2xK3UzTADLPqkTzoZ6sf6cB?tab=summary&timestamp=536.0)
      - [**Runway:** \~2 weeks of content.](https://fathom.video/share/hnjbqbx-s2xK3UzTADLPqkTzoZ6sf6cB?tab=summary&timestamp=536.0)
  - [**Content Feedback:**](https://fathom.video/share/hnjbqbx-s2xK3UzTADLPqkTzoZ6sf6cB?tab=summary&timestamp=552.0)
      - [**Quality:** High; Jason has minimal edits.](https://fathom.video/share/hnjbqbx-s2xK3UzTADLPqkTzoZ6sf6cB?tab=summary&timestamp=552.0)
      - [**Accuracy:** Avoid "overselling" features (e.g., expense reporting) to match current product capabilities.](https://fathom.video/share/hnjbqbx-s2xK3UzTADLPqkTzoZ6sf6cB?tab=summary&timestamp=552.0)
      - [**Process:** Jason's comments will update GrowthX's internal feature artifacts to prevent recurrence.](https://fathom.video/share/hnjbqbx-s2xK3UzTADLPqkTzoZ6sf6cB?tab=summary&timestamp=596.0)

### SEO Performance

  - [**Early Signals:** Impressions are growing, a healthy sign for new content.](https://fathom.video/share/hnjbqbx-s2xK3UzTADLPqkTzoZ6sf6cB?tab=summary&timestamp=748.0)
  - [**Traffic Distribution:** A single article dominates traffic; the goal is to drive impressions and clicks across all articles.](https://fathom.video/share/hnjbqbx-s2xK3UzTADLPqkTzoZ6sf6cB?tab=summary&timestamp=788.0)
  - [**Indexing:** Fixed an issue where Jason's test pages were blocking indexing for 3 live articles.](https://fathom.video/share/hnjbqbx-s2xK3UzTADLPqkTzoZ6sf6cB?tab=summary&timestamp=616.0)

## Next Steps

  - [**GrowthX:**](https://fathom.video/share/hnjbqbx-s2xK3UzTADLPqkTzoZ6sf6cB?tab=summary&timestamp=337.0)
      - [Replace the problematic 5-column table with a simpler format.](https://fathom.video/share/hnjbqbx-s2xK3UzTADLPqkTzoZ6sf6cB?tab=summary&timestamp=337.0)
      - [Update internal feature artifacts using Jason's feedback.](https://fathom.video/share/hnjbqbx-s2xK3UzTADLPqkTzoZ6sf6cB?tab=summary&timestamp=596.0)
      - [Distribute 2 articles to staging today.](https://fathom.video/share/hnjbqbx-s2xK3UzTADLPqkTzoZ6sf6cB?tab=summary&timestamp=536.0)
      - [Deliver 4 articles from production by tomorrow.](https://fathom.video/share/hnjbqbx-s2xK3UzTADLPqkTzoZ6sf6cB?tab=summary&timestamp=536.0)
  - [**Jason:**](https://fathom.video/share/hnjbqbx-s2xK3UzTADLPqkTzoZ6sf6cB?tab=summary&timestamp=685.0)
      - [Continue weekly syncs to maintain alignment.](https://fathom.video/share/hnjbqbx-s2xK3UzTADLPqkTzoZ6sf6cB?tab=summary&timestamp=685.0)

action_items.description: Fix Webflow table layout break; remove min-width or replace w/ bullets/stacked cards
Update feature artifacts w/ Jason's comments; dial back oversold claims in future content
